fields.helpText: This field contains the name of the veteran for whom you are requesting a 2507 exam.
This field contains the date the 2507 request was entered.
This field contains the name of the regional office that entered the request.
This field contains the name of the regional office employee who entered the 2507 request.

This is the date the request was reported (printed) to MAS.  It can only be set one time, at the first printing.  Future prints or reprints of the same request will not alter the "starting" date.  This date is the basis for when the clock begins for both the RO and MAS.   This field is used as part of the implementation of a Nationally Controlled Procedure (AMIS 290).  Per VHA Directive 10-93-142, the information  contained in this field is not to be changed as the results of local (site)  modif

This field contains the date scheduling of the 2507 request was completed through the AMIE option.   This field is used as part of the implementation of a Nationally Controlled Procedure (AMIS 290).  Per VHA Directive 10-93-142, the information  contained in this field is not to be changed as the results of local (site)  modifications.
This field contains the date the entire 2507 request was completed.
This is the date of the last change to the REQUEST STATUS field (#17).   The field is populated by a trigger on the REQUEST STATUS field.

fields.setOfCodes: T:TERMINAL;P:POW;OS:ORIGINAL SC;ON:ORIGINAL NSC;I:INCREASE;R:REVIEW;OTR:OTHER;E:INSUFFICIENT EXAM;AO:AGENT ORANGE;BDD:BEN DELIV AT DISCHG;QS:QUICK START;IDES:IDES;
fields.helpText: This field contains the status of the 2507 exam to be conducted.
This is a list of Claim Types for this request.
This field contains other known disabilities or those stated by the veteran.
This field contains other known disabilities or those stated by the veteran.
This field contains other known disabilities or those stated by the veteran.
This field contains the date/time transcription was completed.
This field contains the date/time exam results were approved.

This field contains the date/time the 2507 request was released to the regional office for printing.   This field is used as part of the implementation of a Nationally Controlled Procedure (AMIS 290).  Per VHA Directive 10-93-142, the information  contained in this field is not to be changed as the results of local (site)  modifications.
This field contains the name of the MAS employee who released the request to the regional office.
This field contains the date/time the examination results were printed by the regional office.
This field contains the name of the regional office employee who printed the examination results.
This field contains the request status of the 2507 exam request.
This field contains the computation of TODAY - DATE REPORTED TO MAS.

This field contains the date/time the entire request was cancelled.   This field is used as part of the implementation of a Nationally Controlled Procedure (AMIS 290).  Per VHA Directive 10-93-142, the information  contained in this field is not to be changed as the results of local (site)  modifications.
This field contains the name of the employee who cancelled the entire request.
This field indicates whether or not a claim folder is required for this request.
An answer is required in the EXAM FOLDER AVAILABLE field if the CLAIM FOLDER REQUIRED field is marked YES

This field indicates whether or not a document will be able to be printed at the regional office.  If YES, the regional office will not be prompted to print the document when it is released by the Medical Center.  option.
This field contains the remarks that are entered for a 2507 request.
This field contains the last date an exam was added to this request.
This field contains the name of the last person to add an exam to this request after it was originally entered by the RO.
This field contains the date the REMARKS field was last modified.
This field contains the name of the employee who last modified the REMARKS field.
This field contains the station name or number of the site which will be responsible for this request.
This field contains the name of the employee who approved the examination results.
This field contains the date/time the exam was approved.
Data moved to File #396.4; field starred for deletion.
This field contains the name of the employee who returned the transferred request to the owner site.
For transferred-in requests, this field contains the domain of the site which owns the request.
This field contains the date of the last rating exam.
This field contains a number which is used to point to the original request at the original site.
This field is set to YES if either the entire request or individual exams on it have been transferred to another site.

This is the date the owner site transferred this request to another site.   This field is used as part of the implementation of a Nationally Controlled Procedure (AMIS 290).  Per VHA Directive 10-93-142, the information  contained in this field is not to be changed as the results of local (site)  modifications.

fields.fieldName: DATE TRANSFRD IN FRM REM SITE
fields.helpText: This field contains the date this transferred in request was received from the owner site.   This field is used as part of the implementation of a Nationally Controlled Procedure (AMIS 290).  Per VHA Directive 10-93-142, the information  contained in this field is not to be changed as the results of local (site)  modifications.

A request may be transferred to multiple sites.  This field represents the date the last transfer on the request was received back at the owner site. Only after this date is set will the request be counted on the AMIS 290.   This field is used as part of the implementation of a Nationally Controlled Procedure (AMIS 290).  Per VHA Directive 10-93-142, the information  contained in this field is not to be changed as the results of local (site)  modifications.
This sub file contains information about the sites where the original request was placed.
This field is a pointer to the insufficient request this 2507 was entered for.
This field contains the processing time of the original request the insufficient request was entered for.
This field is the Request UUID (universally unique identifier for VLER  DAS.)
This is the list of special considerations for this request.
The CAPRI GUI will mark this field Yes or No. If this request is marked  Insufficient, then this field will be set to Y(es) by the CAPRI GUI.
